executiveSummary:
**Executive Summary: Medicaid Program Performance Analysis**

National Medicaid enrollment continues its post-pandemic normalization trend, with 75.3 million enrollees reported as of January 2026, reflecting widespread year-over-year declines as states complete redetermination processes. Most major states experienced enrollment contractions between 3-8%, with Michigan showing the steepest decline at 7.6% and North Carolina as the notable exception with modest growth of 0.5%. Total program expenditures reached $215.9 billion in fiscal year 2024, yielding a national average per-enrollee cost of $2,869. Significant cost variations persist across states, with Pennsylvania ($4,377 per enrollee) and Massachusetts ($4,201 per enrollee) demonstrating substantially higher unit costs compared to Florida ($2,031 per enrollee) and North Carolina ($2,616 per enrollee). The federal government's share represents 65.8% of total program costs at $142.0 billion.

Texas performance presents a mixed picture relative to peer states and national benchmarks. With 4.0 million enrollees experiencing a 3.1% year-over-year decline, Texas mirrors the national enrollment trend while maintaining relatively controlled per-enrollee costs at $2,988—slightly above the national average but well below high-cost states like Pennsylvania and Ohio. However, quality performance remains a concern, with Texas scoring 62 out of 100 and ranking 30th nationally, lagging behind leading states like Massachusetts (81) and Minnesota (80) by substantial margins. Texas demonstrates strength in managed care adoption with 93.3% penetration, exceeding the national average of 83.2%. Recent federal regulatory activity suggests increased scrutiny on state-directed payments and provider enrollment moratoria, areas that warrant continued monitoring given Texas's extensive managed care infrastructure and ongoing procurement activities visible in state contracting portals.
texasSpotlight:
## Texas Medicaid Program Performance Analysis

Texas operates the nation's third-largest Medicaid program with 4.03 million enrollees, demonstrating relatively stable performance during the post-pandemic enrollment transitions. The state's -3.1% year-over-year enrollment decline mirrors the national average and matches Pennsylvania's trajectory, while performing better than large peer states like California (-6.2%) and Michigan (-7.6%). However, Texas significantly trails North Carolina, the only major state showing enrollment growth (+0.5%), highlighting potential gaps in coverage retention strategies. With per-enrollee spending of $2,988—slightly above the national average of $2,869 but well below costly northeastern states—Texas maintains fiscal discipline while serving a population comparable to the entire state of South Carolina.

The state's managed care penetration of 93.3% substantially exceeds the national average of 83.2%, positioning Texas as a leader in care coordination infrastructure with nearly 3.9 million enrollees in managed care plans. However, quality outcomes present a concerning picture: Texas ranks 30th nationally with a quality score of 62/100, placing it in the "moderate" tier but dangerously close to the low-performer threshold of 60. This performance lags behind regional peers and non-expansion states that have achieved higher quality ratings, suggesting that high managed care penetration alone doesn't guarantee superior outcomes. Key areas to monitor include the impact of ongoing federal regulatory changes around state-directed payments, which could affect Texas's financing strategies, and whether the state can leverage its strong managed care foundation to improve quality scores and avoid slipping into the bottom tier of national performers.

alerts.title: Pennsylvania Per-Enrollee Spending 53% Above National Average
alerts.description: Pennsylvania's per-enrollee spending of $4,377 is significantly higher than the national average of $2,869, indicating potential cost efficiency issues.

alerts.title: Michigan Shows Highest Enrollment Decline at -7.6%
alerts.description: Michigan experienced a 7.6% year-over-year enrollment decrease, the steepest decline among major states reported.

alerts.title: Five States Show Critically Low Quality Scores
alerts.description: Arkansas, South Carolina, Alabama, Oklahoma, and Mississippi all scored below 55 on quality measures, well below the national average of 65.6.

alerts.title: Florida Shows Low Per-Enrollee Spending Despite Large Size
alerts.description: Florida's per-enrollee spending of $2,031 is 29% below the national average, potentially indicating efficient operations or possible underfunding.

alerts.title: Nationwide Provider Enrollment Moratorium Active
alerts.description: CMS has announced temporary nationwide moratoriums on provider enrollment, which could impact access to care across all states.

executiveInsights.title: TX Medicaid enrollment declining -3.1% while costs remain $273 above national avg
executiveInsights.summary: Texas Medicaid enrollment dropped 3.1% year-over-year to 4.03M enrollees, yet per-enrollee spending remains at $2,988 versus the national average of $2,869. This creates a dual pressure of shrinking member base with above-average unit costs.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Declining enrollment with sustained high costs suggests potential inefficiencies in care delivery that HHSC must address to maintain program sustainability and demonstrate value.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Flag cost management strategies to HHSC leadership for immediate review

executiveInsights.title: CMS implementing nationwide temporary enrollment moratorium affecting TX
executiveInsights.summary: Federal Register shows CMS announced nationwide temporary moratoria on Medicare, Medicaid, and CHIP enrollments effective in 2026. This directly impacts Texas's 4.03M Medicaid enrollees and managed care operations.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Enrollment freezes will fundamentally alter program dynamics, potentially affecting contract volumes, revenue projections, and operational planning for Accenture's deliverables.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Review Federal Register documents 2026-09717 and 2026-09718 for specific impact details

executiveInsights.title: TX quality score ranks #30 nationally at 62/100, below moderate threshold
executiveInsights.summary: Texas Medicaid quality performance sits at 62/100, ranking 30th nationally and falling in the 'moderate' tier. The state is 13.6 points below the top performers and only 2 points above the low-performer threshold.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Poor quality scores create regulatory risk and limit Texas's ability to secure federal waivers or incentive payments, directly impacting HHSC's strategic priorities.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Flag quality improvement initiatives to HHSC quality team as priority workstream

executiveInsights.title: CMS updating state directed payment rules impacting TX's 93.3% MC program
executiveInsights.summary: New federal regulations on Medicaid managed care state directed payments are being finalized, directly affecting Texas which has 93.3% managed care penetration (3.86M enrollees). This is 10 percentage points above the national average of 83.2%.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Texas's high managed care penetration amplifies the impact of any state directed payment changes, potentially affecting billions in federal funding flows through managed care organizations.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Review Federal Register document 2026-10292 for state directed payment implications

executiveInsights.title: HHS OIG active oversight with ongoing Medicaid portfolio and work plan items
executiveInsights.summary: External signals show HHS OIG maintaining active oversight of Medicaid programs with both a current portfolio of investigations and in-progress work plan items. This indicates heightened federal scrutiny across state Medicaid programs.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Increased OIG activity suggests higher compliance risk for HHSC operations and potential audit exposure for Accenture's contract deliverables and recommendations.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Review OIG Medicaid portfolio and work plan for items affecting Texas operations

riskOpportunity.label: Quality Score Improvement Gap
riskOpportunity.description: Texas ranks 30th nationally with a 62/100 quality score, creating vulnerability in value-based contracts and state performance evaluations.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Develop comprehensive quality improvement roadmap targeting top 10 state ranking within 24 months.

riskOpportunity.label: Enrollment Decline Impact
riskOpportunity.description: Texas Medicaid enrollment dropped 3.1% year-over-year, potentially reducing contract values and operational scale.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Model revenue impact scenarios and develop contingency plans for continued enrollment decline.

riskOpportunity.label: State Directed Payments Reform
riskOpportunity.description: New federal regulations on Medicaid managed care state directed payments could reshape funding mechanisms and contract structures.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Establish regulatory response team to analyze implications and position for emerging funding opportunities.

riskOpportunity.label: HHS OIG Oversight Intensification
riskOpportunity.description: Active OIG oversight docket and work plan items signal increased scrutiny of Medicaid operations and compliance requirements.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Conduct comprehensive compliance audit and strengthen internal controls documentation immediately.

riskOpportunity.label: Managed Care Contract Recompete
riskOpportunity.description: Texas's 93.3% managed care penetration creates significant contract renewal opportunities given the state's high engagement model.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Launch competitive positioning analysis and capability enhancement initiative for upcoming procurement cycles.

riskOpportunity.label: Provider Enrollment Moratorium
riskOpportunity.description: Nationwide temporary moratorium on provider enrollment could disrupt network adequacy and service delivery models.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Assess network impact and develop alternative service delivery strategies for affected provider categories.

riskOpportunity.label: Per-Enrollee Cost Efficiency
riskOpportunity.description: Texas's $2,988 per-enrollee cost is above national average but below peer states, indicating potential for value-based optimization.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Design cost reduction initiatives targeting 15% efficiency gains while maintaining quality outcomes.

riskOpportunity.label: Technology Modernization Procurement
riskOpportunity.description: Active Texas procurement opportunities signal potential system upgrades and digital transformation initiatives across HHSC operations.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Establish dedicated pursuit team to monitor and respond to emerging technology RFPs from Texas HHSC.

pulseMetrics.label: Total Enrollment
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Texas has 4,032,864 Medicaid enrollees as of January 2026.

pulseMetrics.label: Enrollment Change
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Texas enrollment decreased 3.1% year-over-year, consistent with national unwinding trends.

pulseMetrics.label: Per Enrollee Spend
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Texas spends $2,988 per Medicaid enrollee annually, below the national average of $2,869.

pulseMetrics.label: Managed Care Rate
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Texas has 93.3% managed care penetration with 3,863,808 enrollees in managed care plans.

pulseMetrics.label: Quality Score
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Texas ranks 30th nationally with a quality score of 62, categorized as moderate performance.

pulseMetrics.label: Active Procurements
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Three active procurement items identified across Texas HHSC and ESBD portals.

pulseMetrics.label: Regulatory Signals
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Eleven active regulatory and OIG oversight items affecting Texas Medicaid operations.

pulseMetrics.label: Next Milestone
pulseMetrics.tooltip: No specific milestone dates available in current data sources.
